sutta: the buddha said monks 1 might say speaking rightly of nanda he is a clansman 1 might say he is strong 1 might say he is handsome 1 might say speaking rightly of him he is very passionate monks unless he keep the doors of the senses guarded be moderate in eating be watchful or wakeful unless he have mindfulness and self and awareness how can nanda live the holy life in all its purity monks this is the way for nanda to guard the doors of the senses monks if nanda has occasion to look to the eastern water concentrating his whole attention he gazes there at reflecting thus while i look to the eastern water they shall not flow in upon me covetousness nor discontent nor evil nor any unwholesome state thus in this way is he aware if he has occasion to look to the western quarter to the northern quarter to the southern quarter to the zenith to the nader to the points between the quarters concentrating his whole attention he gazes there at reflecting thus while i look to the points between the quarters covetousness and the rest shall not flow in upon me thus in this way is he aware monks this is the way for nanda to guard the doors of the senses monks this is the way of ananda to be moderate in eating monks herein nanda eats his food with orderly reflection not for amusement for lust for adornment for beauty but to suffice for the sustaining and nourishment of his body to appease hunger and to aid the practice of the holy life he reflects in this way i shall crush out the old feelings and not allow any new feelings to arise and i shall continue to dwell in blamelessness and comfort monks this is the way for nanda to be mounted in eating monks this is the way for nanda to watch fullness or wakefulness monks herein by day nanda purges his mind of thoughts that hinder when walking when sitting down so too during the first watch of the night during the middle watch he lies like a lion on his right side with foot covering foot mindful and aware intent upon the thought of getting up getting up in the last watch of the night he purges his mind of thoughts that hinder when walking and when sitting monks this is the way for nanda to watchfulness monks this is the way of ananda to mindfulness and awareness monks hearing to nanda recognize feelings rise recognize the endo recognize they said recognize perceptions rise recognize the endo recognize they said recognize reflections rise recognize the endo recognize they said monks this is the way for nanda to mindfulness and awareness monks unless nanda keep the doors of the senses guarded be moderate in eating be watchful unless you have mindfulness and awareness how can nanda live the holy life in all its purity as the end of the suta
commentary: so we see in describing nanda who is supposed to be a half brother i think of the buddha the buddha said he's strong he's handsome he's very passionate so unless he keep he practice these few things unless he keep the doors of the senses guarded be moderate in eating be watchful or wakeful and have mindfulness and awareness it will be difficult for nanda to live the holy life in all its purity and concerning these few things that he has to practice for watchfulness or wakefulness and buddha said he purges his mind of thoughts that hinder and walking when sitting and then during the first watch of the night during the middle watch the first watch of the night is the time from 6 to 10 pm the middle watch is from 10 pm to 2 am and the last watch is from 2 am to 6 am and then the last part is for ananda to practice satisfaction you notice here the description of satisfaction is a little different from a few other sutas like in the satipatthana and the buddha describes satishan pajanya mindfulness and awareness the buddha described being aware of the bodily actions means when he's walking he knows he's walking when he's sitting he knows he's sitting when he's eating he's aware he's eating when he's changing his clothes he knows that he's changing his clothes or his ropes and whatever other bodily action but here the buddha is talking about mindfulness and awareness of the mind the working of the mind recognize feelings rise recognize the endo recognize they said recognize perceptions rise recognize they endure recognize they said recognize reflections or thoughts rise recognize the endure recognize they said so in other words here the other aspect of mindfulness and awareness is the watching the mind and in fact watching the mind is more important than the uh bodily actions uh when we are aware of the bodily actions it just helps to keep us mindful so that our mind is not scattered and but here when feelings perceptions and reflections or thoughts rise then we recognize them we are aware that we always have to remember concerning the body and the mind that these 5 kandas these 5 aggregates are not i not mine not myself so if you recognize these feelings these perceptions and these thoughts and we recognize them as not i not mine and not myself then we don't get uh we don't we we are not moved by them we are not swayed by them we adopt uh like uh an observer attitude now we just observe it with equinum equanimity then we see things as we as they really are instead of getting moved by the eu states yeah and now we come to the nexuta
